# Dense Printer

Dense Printer packs markdown onto as few printed 8.5x11in (US Letter) pages as
possible: multi-column layout, small-but-legible type, trimmed margins. Use it
when a person asks to print notes, docs, or reference material compactly.

HTML works too: pass it via the `html` URL parameter (below), open/drop .html
files, paste rich text or raw HTML source, or drag a browser selection. All of
it is converted to markdown client-side (Turndown + GFM tables) before layout;
scripts, styles, nav, and form chrome are stripped during conversion.

App URL: https://avi-perl.github.io/dense-printer/

## URL API (no server, no auth)

Pass the document and settings in the URL fragment. The app renders fully
paginated pages on load; nothing is sent to any server (fragments stay
client-side) and nothing is persisted.

    https://avi-perl.github.io/dense-printer/#md=<BASE64>&size=8&cols=3

- `md`: the markdown, UTF-8, base64-encoded. Standard or URL-safe base64 both
  accepted. Provide `md` or `html` (one is required); `md` wins if both.
- `html`: HTML instead of markdown, UTF-8, base64-encoded (same base64 rules).
  Converted to markdown client-side on load — useful when you have a web page
  or HTML fragment and don't want to convert it yourself.
- `name`: document title shown in the footer area. Default `Linked.md`.
- `size`: font size in points, 3-24. Default 9.
- `cols`: columns per page, 1-4. Default 3.
- `line`: line height, 1-1.8. Default 1.2.
- `margin`: page margins in inches, 0.1-1. Default 0.25.
- `para`: paragraph spacing in em, 0-1. Default 0.35.
- `scale`: overall squeeze percentage, 25-120. Default 100.
- `font`: `serif` | `sans` | `compact` | `micro`. Default `micro`.
  `compact` and `micro` fit the most text; `micro` stays legible at tiny sizes.
- `headings`, `justify`, `footer`, `staple`: `1` or `0`. Defaults: headings=1,
  justify=1, footer=1, staple=0. `staple` reserves a clear strip for a staple.
- `autofill`: `1` to auto-maximize the type after load — grows the font size
  (then the squeeze scale) until any bigger would add a page, so a half-empty
  last page gets absorbed into larger, more readable type. Runs after web
  fonts load; starts from whatever `size`/`scale` you passed. Also available
  interactively as the "Auto fill" toolbar button.

A dense starting point for maximum text per sheet:
`size=7&cols=3&line=1.1&margin=0.15&font=compact&justify=1`
Add `&autofill=1` to grow the type back up to exactly fill the page count.

## Font & size test sheet

    https://avi-perl.github.io/dense-printer/test.html

A one-page printable specimen: all four fonts (serif, sans, compact, micro)
at sizes from 12pt down to 3pt, at the app's default 1.2 line spacing. Its
purpose: on-paper legibility can't be judged from a screen, so the person
prints this sheet once, reads down each font, and the smallest row they can
still read comfortably is their personal minimum.

How to help a person choose a size:
- If they haven't calibrated, suggest printing the test sheet first (open the
  URL and print, or use the headless recipe below on this page). Ask them for
  the smallest (font, size) pair they found comfortable, then use it in every
  Dense Printer link you generate for them.
- Reasonable priors while uncalibrated: 9-10pt serif/sans reads like a normal
  document; 7-8pt compact suits dense reference material; micro is designed
  for tiny sizes and most people still read it at 5-6pt. Eyesight, printer
  quality, and paper vary — the sheet beats any guess.
- Remember their answer across sessions if you can; it's a stable personal
  constant worth reusing.

## Getting a PDF without a visible browser

The page needs a browser layout engine to paginate, but a headless one works.
Wait for web fonts and pagination to settle before printing.

Headless Chrome one-liner (bash):

    chrome --headless=new --print-to-pdf=out.pdf --no-pdf-header-footer \
      --virtual-time-budget=8000 \
      "https://avi-perl.github.io/dense-printer/#md=$(base64 -w0 notes.md)&size=8&cols=3"

Playwright:

    const md = Buffer.from(markdown, "utf8").toString("base64url");
    const page = await browser.newPage();
    await page.goto(`https://avi-perl.github.io/dense-printer/#md=${md}&size=8&cols=3`);
    await page.waitForSelector("#pages .page");
    await page.evaluate(() => document.fonts.ready);
    await page.waitForTimeout(200); // pagination debounce (use ~500ms with autofill=1)
    await page.pdf({ path: "out.pdf", format: "Letter", printBackground: true, preferCSSPageSize: true });

Notes:
- Very large documents make very long URLs. Browsers handle multi-hundred-KB
  fragments, but OS command-line argument limits (~32KB on Windows) can
  truncate the Chrome one-liner; use the Playwright route for big documents.
- For interactive use, just open the URL — the person sees the laid-out pages
  and can tweak settings in the toolbar before printing.
